texoclaim-chain memory
agentledgerdrift
stream

claim-chain memory · append-only · receipts on every answer

Memory that knows
when to stop believing things.

Every fact you state becomes a journaled claim. When a fact changes, the old claim isn't buried in a vector index — it'sstruck, sealed with a receipt, and superseded on the record. Git tracks code diffs. texo tracks claim diffs.

journal · canonicalfrontier —trace a claim ↓
the map

The journey of a claim.

Every fact you state walks the same pipeline. The heuristic floor and the Qwen judge decide, per claim, whether it lands as new truth, retires an older claim, or opens a conflict — and the record keeps all three.

the replay

Replay the frontier.

A real session, unspooled. Scroll to move the tape under the playhead — facts land, changed ones are struck and sealed with a receipt as the new truth rises, contradictions open as conflicts. Scroll back and it all reverses: you're scrubbing the record.

  1. seq 03

    The platform uses Postgres for storage.

    old_arch.md:8

    current
  2. seq 05

    Deploys happen on Friday.

    onboarding.md:5

    current
  3. seq 09

    Alice owns release approval.

    meeting_notes.md:3

    current
  4. seq 14

    Deploys happen on Friday.

    retired · receipt 480bbd6c

    Deploys moved to Wednesday.

    adr_007.md:14 · current

  5. seq 15

    Alice owns release approval.

    retired · receipt 42abd965

    Ben owns release approval now.

    meeting_notes.md:5 · current

  6. seq 18

    Release sign-off is verbal.

    ⚔ contradicts

    Release sign-off must be in the ticket.

    open conflict
  7. seq 22

    The platform uses Postgres for storage.

    retired · receipt 0ad49b50

    The platform uses BatPak for append-only event storage.

    architecture.md:3 · current

  8. seq 24

    Onboarding uses the current checklist.

    current_architecture.md:9

    current

The heuristic floor can't separate “Alice owns release approval” from “Ben owns it now”— same subject, different words. The Qwen relation judge retires the superseded claim and surfaces genuine contradictions asconflicts instead of silently trusting the newer sentence. Everything above is real: real chains, real receipt addresses, real sequence numbers.

the live ledger

Current truth, stale record, open conflicts.

Replayed from the journal at this frontier. Backend offline — showing the seeded ledger.

current0
    stale0
      conflict0
        teach the ledger—open
        1. State a fact. Then state one that changes it. Then end session — the relation judge retires the old claim and it re-appears struck on the stale rail, with a receipt.
        try:
        the party trick

        texo audits its own docs.

        Feed the repo's own prose back through the pipeline and its stale architecture claims come back superseded — with receipts — by the docs that replaced them. The thesis, demonstrated on the tool.

        see the drift →
        texo · claim-chain memoryevery answer carries its receipts
        memory · live
        frontier
        —
        current
        —
        stale
        —
        conflict
        —
        settlement
        —
